reason: The remediation modifies GRUB2 boot loader configuration by changing the superuser account name and regenerating grub.cfg. Errors can render the bootloader password protection ineffective or cause boot-time editing failures, posing a high risk to system recovery. According to policy, bootloader configuration changes are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; bootloader misconfiguration; loss of GRUB superuser access; potential inability to edit boot entries; need to regenerate grub.cfg correctly
